confirmed_note: Verified 2026-07-19. Camera confirmed static for the whole clip BEFORE calibrating (full-clip 2-column pixel scan, no drift found -- see reference_video3_calibration.py docstring), so one static homography covers all 933 frames. All 4 corners independently re-measured at frames 0, 466 (mid-clip), and 932 (last frame) via numeric pixel-brightness thresholding (not eyeballed grid crops) -- BL reads (193,841)/(196,837)/(196,837) and TR reads (1336,268)/(1335,267)/(1335,267) across the 3 frames, small real variance, consistent with the confirmed-static camera. Rendered overlay visually confirmed tight at all 4 corners on all 3 frames (calib_check_frame0/466/932.jpg in cv_pipeline/scratch_output/tennis3/calib_verify/manifest_frames/). NOTE: the initial calibration pass (eyeballed crop-grid reads) had a near_svc_L outlier residual of 22px vs 3.5-8.8px for the other 7 points -- investigated like a potential mislabeling bug (wide crop first), confirmed the line identity was correct, root-caused as plain eyeballing imprecision, and fixed by switching to numeric brightness-thresholding for all 8 points, dropping held-out error to 1.68px/1.68px (best of any clip so far) with uniform 1.47-4.61px residuals, no outliers.
